ttl.name: VEHICLE STABILITY CONTROL SYSTEM (for BOSCH Made)

para.dtcname: Lost Communication with Yaw Rate Sensor Module
Lost Communication with Steering Angle Sensor Module

content5.ptxt: The skid control ECU receives the signals from the ECM, steering angle sensor, and yaw rate and acceleration sensor via the CAN communication system.

entry.ptxt: When either of the following is detected:

item.ptxt: Bus OFF state continues for 300 msec.
The ECU detects that it cannot send a signal.

entry.ptxt: When either of the following is detected:

item.ptxt: At a vehicle speed of 4 mph (6 km/h) or more, the skid control ECU receives data that is out of range from the engine.
At a vehicle speed of 4 mph (6 km/h) or more, the skid control ECU receives abnormal data from the engine (5 cycles).

entry.ptxt: When any r of the following is detected:

item.ptxt: The skid control ECU receives an abnormal signal from the yaw rate sensor (5 cycles).
The skid control ECU receives a signal that has a shorter message than normal.
The skid control ECU cannot receive a signal from the yaw rate sensor.

entry.ptxt: (Skid control ECU to yaw rate and acceleration sensor)
When any of the following is detected:

item.ptxt: The skid control ECU receives an abnormal signal from the steering angle sensor (5 cycles).
The skid control ECU receives a signal that has a shorter message than normal.
The skid control ECU cannot receive a signal from the steering angle sensor.

entry.ptxt: (Skid control ECU to steering angle sensor)

content5.ptxt: Proceed to CAN communication system troubleshooting (See page ).

atten4.ptxt: When a DTC indicating a CAN communication system malfunction is output, repair the CAN communication system before repairing each corresponding sensor.
